quote: Among the most reliable and skilled electrophysiologists we have worked with — patient outcomes consistently above the regional benchmark.

letter.org: Liverpool Heart and Chest Hospital NHS Foundation Trust
letter.subtitle: The largest specialist cardiothoracic centre in the United Kingdom
body: It gives me great pleasure to write in support of Professor Dhiraj Gupta, who has been a consultant cardiologist and electrophysiologist at this Trust since 2007.

Over the past seventeen years, Professor Gupta has consistently been among the most reliable and skilled electrophysiologists we have worked with. His patient outcomes are consistently above the regional benchmark, his complication rates have been published in peer-reviewed journals, and his teaching of junior staff is exemplary.

He has been a key driver of the LAA occlusion service which now serves a population of 2.8 million across the North West of England. He is, without reservation, a senior figure in UK arrhythmia care, and we are fortunate to have him.
letter.sigRole: Liverpool Heart and Chest Hospital NHS Foundation Trust

quote: Professor Gupta's leadership of our LAA occlusion service has set the standard others now measure themselves against.

letter.subtitle: Institute of Cardiovascular Medicine and Science
body: Professor Gupta heads the arrhythmia work group of the Institute, a research collaboration between Royal Brompton and Liverpool Heart and Chest. His leadership has set the standard others now measure themselves against.

In the four years he has chaired the group, the team has published in Heart, JACC, Europace and the European Heart Journal — and has secured grant funding well in excess of three million pounds for arrhythmia research.
His work on left atrial appendage occlusion is, in my view, the single most important contribution to the field from a UK centre in the last decade.
letter.sigRole: Institute of Cardiovascular Medicine and Science

quote: His proctoring has materially raised the quality of complex ablation work across our cardiology unit.

letter.subtitle: Department of Cardiology
body: I am writing to confirm that Professor Gupta has acted as a proctor for our complex ablation programme on multiple occasions over the past five years.

His proctoring has materially raised the quality of complex ablation work across our cardiology unit. Beyond the technical instruction, he models a thoughtful, patient-first approach which our junior consultants have learned a great deal from.
We refer cases to him in Liverpool with complete confidence.

quote: A clinician of rare calibre — operating at the intersection of high-volume practice, rigorous research and patient-centred care.
detail: Faculty of Health and Life Sciences

letter.subtitle: Faculty of Health and Life Sciences
body: Professor Gupta holds an honorary professorship of cardiology at this university, awarded in recognition of more than three hundred and fifty peer-reviewed scientific publications, citation counts in excess of thirteen thousand, and approximately five million pounds in competitive research grants secured throughout his career.

He is, in our judgment, a clinician of rare calibre — operating at the intersection of high-volume practice, rigorous research and patient-centred care. The university is proud to count him among its faculty.
letter.sigRole: Faculty of Health and Life Sciences, University of Liverpool

quote: His contribution as Senior Lecturer has shaped how a generation of trainees approach atrial fibrillation.

letter.subtitle: National Heart and Lung Institute
body: Professor Gupta has been a Senior Lecturer at Imperial College London since 2011. His contribution as Senior Lecturer has shaped how a generation of trainees approach atrial fibrillation.
His teaching is widely cited by trainees as a highlight of the cardiology curriculum, and his research mentorship has produced multiple successful MD and PhD candidates over the years.
It is no exaggeration to say that the quality of UK arrhythmia training has been measurably improved by his involvement.
letter.sigRole: National Heart and Lung Institute, Imperial College London

quote: Professor Gupta's published complication rate places him firmly in the top echelon of UK electrophysiologists.

letter.subtitle: Council statement
letter.date: Issued 2018, on conclusion of council term
body: Professor Gupta served on the Council of the British Heart Rhythm Society from 2015 to 2018, a period during which the society significantly expanded its training and quality-assurance remit.

Professor Gupta's published complication rate places him firmly in the top echelon of UK electrophysiologists. His advocacy on Council was characterised by a relentless focus on procedural quality and patient safety — a focus that has shaped society guidance issued since.
The society is grateful for his service and continues to regard him as a leader in the field.

quote: After years of being told my palpitations were "just stress," Professor Gupta listened, ran proper tests, and treated me. I haven't had an episode in two years.
He explained my AF in a way I could finally understand. Three languages, no jargon, no rush. The procedure went exactly as he described.
My father is 76 and we were terrified. The professor took an hour with us — an hour. He treated my dad like a person, not a case number.
Genuinely the kindest doctor I have ever met. And the best — my flutter is gone after eight years of beta-blockers that never quite worked.
I came from Delhi for my consultation expecting a cold London hospital. Got something completely different — felt like sitting with a family doctor who happens to be world-class.
My ablation was nine months ago. Heart rhythm has been steady since the day I went home. I'd waited fourteen years for this.
He spoke to my mother in Hindi and to me in English in the same conversation. That seems like a small thing — until you've spent years translating for an elderly parent in clinics.
Followed up with me by email three weeks after the procedure to check how I was. Three weeks. Unprompted.
Dhiraj is among the operators I would refer my own family to without a second thought. His judgement on borderline cases is exceptional.
One of the most thoughtful electrophysiologists in Britain. His VISTAX trial work has changed how a number of us approach pulmonary vein isolation.
Combines the rigour of a researcher with the calm of a senior surgeon. The patients I send him come back better — and remember the experience.
Working with Dhiraj on multicentre trials has been the highlight of my decade. He raises the standard of every team he sits in.
Genuinely one of the few clinicians whose name brings my whole arrhythmia MDT to attention when his cases come up.
A consummate clinician. The thing that distinguishes Dhiraj is how seriously he takes the question after the question — what really matters to this patient.

headline: Global heart rhythm expert conducts free training at PGIMER
quote: I am using the opportunity to perform complex cases in five cities and train local cardiologists in these procedures.

headline: UK cardiologist kicks off India tour from PGIMER
quote: Performing the costly procedures without taking "a single paisa" from patients or hospitals — even bearing his own travel expenses.

headline: UK Heart Rhythm Expert Begins India Tour from Chd
quote: A philanthropist and proud alumnus of PGIMER Chandigarh and AIIMS New Delhi… reflecting his commitment to expanding access to advanced cardiac care.

headline: Global heart expert to give free treatment, train doctors across India
quote: As a proud alumnus of PGIMER Chandigarh and AIIMS New Delhi, I have retained strong professional and personal links with India.
